summary: The imbalance conjecture remains open. Partial results confirm that $M_G$ is graphic for trees, unicyclic graphs, antiregular graphs, complete extensions of paths/cycles/complete graphs, and several classes of block graphs. The most recent work (Kozerenko–Serdiuk, Opuscula Math. 2023) substantially extends this list but does not resolve the general conjecture.

since_posted.title: Edge Imbalance Sequences And Their Graphicness
since_posted.claim: Proves that certain unary and binary graph operations preserve graphicness of imbalance sequences, and discusses several conjectures related to graphicness, including connections between them.

since_posted.title: New results on imbalance graphic graphs
since_posted.claim: Establishes imbalance graphicness for unicyclic graphs, antiregular graphs, and three classes of block graphs, extending earlier results but leaving the general conjecture open.

search_queries: Kozerenko imbalance conjecture graphic sequence graph theory
edge imbalance multiset graphic sequence graph conjecture
notes: The original OPG bibliography paper 'On graphs with graphic imbalance sequences' (Kozerenko–Skochko, Algebra Discrete Math. 2014) is the starting-point reference and is excluded from since_posted. No counterexample has been found; the conjecture has been verified for all graphs with ≤9 vertices. No arXiv preprints specifically about the main imbalance conjecture were found. The 2019 paper's DOI could not be confirmed from the KSE publications page alone.
